C0.restriction: Over individuals OK · never over assemblies

C1.restriction: No expected overflight of uninvolved persons

C2.restriction: Low-speed mode (≤ 3 m/s) → 5 m · normal → 30 m

C3.restriction: ≥ 150 m from inhabited areas · ≥ 30 m or 1:1 from people

C4.restriction: No autonomous modes — manual control only

C5.restriction: VLOS, controlled ground area, airspace observers

C6.restriction: BVLOS over sparsely populated · observer chain required

whCalculator.heading: Interactive · Wh calculator
whCalculator.capacityHint: Per-cell pack — read from the battery label
whCalculator.formula: Formula: Wh = (mAh ÷ 1000) × cells × 3.7 V nominal. Below 100 Wh: free. 100–160 Wh: airline approval. Above 160 Wh: forbidden in passenger cabin.

imsafe.heading: Interactive · IMSAFE self-check
imsafe.verdictWaiting: Answer all six to see the verdict
imsafe.verdictGo: GO — all six clear. Run the UAS and environment checks next.
imsafe.verdictNoGo: NO-GO — one or more risks active. Postpone or delegate.

illness.label: Illness
illness.prompt: Any active infection, cold, or symptoms affecting concentration?

medication.label: Medication
medication.prompt: Any medication that may cause drowsiness or affect alertness?

stress.label: Stress
stress.prompt: Acute emotional stress (argument, bad news, financial pressure)?

alcohol.label: Alcohol
alcohol.prompt: Any alcohol consumed in the last 8 hours?

fatigue.label: Fatigue
fatigue.prompt: Fewer than 6 hours of sleep last night, or sustained sleep debt?

eating.label: Eating
eating.prompt: No food in the last 4 hours, or feeling hypoglycaemic?

zoneClassifier.heading: Interactive · classify the zone
zoneClassifier.intro: Decide which UGZ type each scenario falls into. Tap to reveal.

z1.scenario: A bird nesting reserve on a small island. The CAA map marks it 'active during nesting season — please avoid'. No active restriction on the date you plan to fly.
z1.explanation: Information zones carry no legal restriction — they exist to warn the pilot of nearby sensitivity. Flying is allowed without any application.

z2.scenario: A patch of airspace adjacent to Riga airport's CTR. The map says 'flight permitted with BGKIS application — max 50 m AGL'.
z2.explanation: Restricted zones require an application and approval. Since 2025-01-01 BGKIS is the mandatory channel. The ceiling stated in the approval (50 m here) takes precedence over the general 120 m.

z3.scenario: EVR17 Border East — the full eastern border strip with Russia and Belarus, since 2025.
z3.explanation: Prohibited zones are not flyable in Open category at all. EVR17 specifically carries criminal liability, not just an administrative fine.

z4.scenario: An active military training area marked 'No flight 08:00–18:00 weekdays during May'. The day you plan to fly is a Saturday in June.
z4.explanation: Outside the active window the zone is effectively information-only. Always re-check the zone status against the date and time of the planned flight, not against the season as a whole.

z5.scenario: A nuclear research facility's perimeter. The map shows a red filled polygon labelled 'no UAS flight permitted'.
z5.explanation: Critical infrastructure with a hard no-fly designation. No application can lift the prohibition for Open category.

distanceRule.heading: Interactive · distance from uninvolved persons

a1-c0.label: A1 — C0 (<250 g)
a1-c0.note: Overflight of individuals OK · never over assemblies

a1-c1.label: A1 — C1 (<900 g)
a1-c1.note: Plan around people · no expected overflight

a2-low.label: A2 — C2 low-speed (≤ 3 m/s)
a2-low.note: Activate low-speed mode BEFORE closing to 5 m

distanceRule.a3NoteFloor: 30 m floor applies — 1:1 not yet binding
distanceRule.a3NoteActive: 1:1 rule active · {altitude} m altitude → {altitude} m distance
distanceRule.footer: Remember: the 1:1 rule in A3 means horizontal distance ≥ flight altitude, with a 30 m floor. The crowd prohibition applies in every sub-category regardless of altitude or distance.
